articles.title: Morning update
articles.summary: Morning update Good morning Middle East Eye readers, The funeral procession for Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has begun in Tehran, with state media reporting the attendance of millions.  The procession in the capital is expected to last for 10 to 12 hours. His body will later be taken through the city of Qom on Tuesday, then to Iraq on Wednesday, before returning to the city of Mashhad on Thursday for burial. Here are some of the other latest developments across the region:  Iran will fully close Tehran’s airspace on Monday as part of funeral arrangements, the Iranian

Iran state media says millions attending former Supreme Leader's funeral procession
articles.summary: Iran state media says millions attending former Supreme Leader's funeral procession Iranian media is reporting that millions have gathered on the streets of its capital to mourn the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. According to Press TV, officials have described the event as the "largest public gathering in the country’s modern history". General Hassan Hassanzadeh, a senior commander in Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, urged the public to "go peacefully to Azadi Square". Rafael Nadal's Retirement Is So Great He Doesn't Miss Tennis
articles.summary: Francine Lacqua speaks with tennis legend Rafael Nadal about the mindset that fueled his extraordinary career. They explore how resilience, unwavering determination and even self-doubt drove him to overcome repeated injuries and become one of tennis's greatest champions. Nadal also opens up about life after retirement, explaining why he still needs goals every day as he builds businesses, grows his academy, and explores what's next beyond the court. (Source: Bloomberg)

Israeli attacks on residential area, water station kill at least four in Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli attacks on residential area, water station kill at least four in Gaza At least four Palestinians were killed and several others were injured as a result of Israeli strikes and shelling across the Gaza Strip early on Monday.  In one Israeli attack, an air strike targeted a residential apartment south of Gaza City, resulting in the killing of two. Another bombing east of the city that struck a gathering of displaced people in front of a water refilling station killed two others.

CarbonScape COO on CATL Investment & Partnership
articles.summary: Vincent Ledoux-Pedailles, Chief Commercial Officer at CarbonScape, discusses the deal that will see Chinese battery giant CATL and Hong Kong–based Lochpine Capital take a 20% strategic stake in the company. CarbonScape converts forestry byproducts into graphite for lithium batteries. He says the partnership will help scale CarbonScape’s technology over the next two years, with commercial production targeted for 2029–2030. He speaks exclusively with Haidi Stroud Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian teen in the West Bank
articles.summary: Israeli forces kill Palestinian teen in the West Bank A Palestinian teenager on Sunday was killed by Israeli forces after he was gunned down during a raid on the Qalandiya refugee camp. The Palestinian Health Ministry identified the 16 year-old teenager as Walid Nidal Abu Sneineh. Two other children were wounded in the same raid. Sneineh is among over 1,175 Palestinians killed by Israeli forces and settlers in the occupied territory since 7 October, 2023.
